LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF BULLIVANT HOUSER BAILEY PC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS MET ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCING ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE ON EACH ELEMENT OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ACCOUNT STATED. (CCP SEC. 437C(P)(1).) THE ELEMENTS OF A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR ACCOUNT STATED ARE: (1) PREVIOUS TRANSACTIONS BETWEEN THE PARTIES ESTABLISHING THE RELATIONSHIP OF DEBTOR AND CREDITOR; (2) AN AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, ON THE AMOUNT DUE FROM THE DEBTOR TO THE CREDITOR; AND (3) A PROMISE BY THE DEBTOR, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, TO PAY THE AMOUNT DUE. (ZINN V. FRED R. BRIGHT CO. (1969) 271 CAL.APP.2D 597, 600.) AS TO THE FIRST ELEMENT, PLAINTIFF HAS PRODUCED ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT THE PARTIES ENTERED INTO AN ENGAGEMENT LETTER SETTING FORTH PAYMENT TERMS. (DECLARATION OF EDWARD WINCHESTER, EX. A.) PLAINTIFF HAS ALSO PRODUCED EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANT PAID THE FIRST TWO INVOICES AND PARTIALLY MADE PAYMENT ON A THIRD. (DECLARATION OF RON KINONEN, PAR. 3.) AS TO THE SECOND ELEMENT, AN AGREEMENT BETWEEN PARTIES TO ESTABLISH AN ACCOUNT STATED "COMES ABOUT BY THE CREDITOR RENDERING A STATEMENT OF THE ACCOUNT TO THE DEBTOR." (ZINN, 271 CAL.APP.2D AT 600.) PLAINTIFF HAS PRODUCED EVIDENCE SHOWING PLAINTIFF REGULARLY SENT INVOICES TO DEFENDANT. (KINONEN DECL., PAR. 2, EX. E; SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION OF RON KINONEN, PAR. 2-3, EXS. I-L.) AS TO THE THIRD ELEMENT, PLAINTIFF HAS PRODUCED ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SHOWING THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT OBJECT TO THE BILLS (EXCEPT FOR ONE WHICH THE PARTIES AGREED TO SETTLE THE AMOUNT) BY WRITING WITHIN 30 DAYS OF RECEIPT, AS REQUIRED BY THE ENGAGEMENT LETTER. (KINONEN DECL., PAR. 4.) "WHEN A STATEMENT IS RENDERED TO A DEBTOR AND NO REPLY IS MADE IN A REASONABLE TIME, THE LAW IMPLIES AN AGREEMENT THAT THE ACCOUNT IS CORRECT AS RENDERED." (MAGGIO, INC. V. NEAL (1987) 196 CAL.APP.3D 745, 753.) THE EVI

Off Calendar Jul-18-2014 Continued To Oct-01-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-21-2014 Set For Case Management Conference On Aug-06-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-04-2014 Unknown
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Edward D. Winchester, Esq. of Bullivant Houser Bailey, Tel. 415.352.2713, for plaintiff and moving party;
T.G. Chilton, Jr., Esq. of Chilton Law Offices, Tel. 602.291.5800, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF BULLIVANT HOUSER BAILEY PC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Plaintiff has met its burden of producing admissible evidence on each element of the cause of action for account stated. (CCP sec. 437c(p)(1).) The elements of a cause of action for account stated are: (1) Previous transactions between the parties establishing the relationship of debtor and creditor; (2) an agreement between the parties, express or implied, on the amount due from the debtor to the creditor; and (3) a promise by the debtor, express or implied, to pay the amount due. (Zinn v. Fred R. Bright Co. (1969) 271 Cal.App.2d 597, 600.) As to the first element, Plaintiff has produced admissible evidence showing that the parties entered into an engagement letter setting forth payment terms. (Declaration of Edward Winchester, Ex. A.) Plaintiff has also produced evidence establishing that Defendant paid the first two invoices and partially made payment on a third. (Declaration of Ron Kinonen, par. 3.) As to the second element, an agreement between parties to establish an account stated "comes about by the creditor rendering a statement of the account to the debtor." (Zinn, 271 Cal.App.2d at 600.) Plaintiff has produced evidence showing Plaintiff regularly sent invoices to Defendant. (Kinonen Decl., par. 2, Ex. E; Supplemental Declaration of Ron Kinonen, par. 2-3, exs. I-L.) As to the third element, Plaintiff has produced admissible evidence showing that Defendant did not object to the bills (except for one which the parties agreed to settle the amount) by writing within 30 days of receipt, as required by the engagement letter. (Kinonen Decl., par. 4.) "When a statement is rendered to a debtor and no reply is made in a reasonable time, the law implies an agreement that the account is correct as rendered." (Maggio, Inc. v. Neal (1987) 196 Cal.App.3d 745, 753.) The evidence also establishes that Defendant agreed to settle the disputed bill for $33,036. (Kinonen Decl., par. 5, Ex. F.) Consequently, the burden shifted to Defendant to establish a triable issue of material fact. (CCP sec. 437c(p)(1).) Defendant has not met its burden. Defendant has not produced admissible evidence showing that it objected to the invoices in writing within 30 days of receipt, as required by the engagement letter. Defendant has not produced admissible evidence showing that it entered into the settlement agreement under duress. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312 and CCP 437c(g).) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
